extracted_from: DISCUSSION_SUMMARY.md §5 (8 rules learned from comparing bot SQL vs IDRE Prisma source) + report_reference_cards.json (13 card triggers)
description: Universal cross-cutting rules that apply to any query matching their keywords, plus per-report card triggers. Loaded by platform_context_agent and surfaced into state['platform_context'] for the sql_writer prompt. Matching algorithm: case-insensitive substring (`kw.lower() in query_lower`). Tune keyword granularity accordingly — single-word keywords match aggressively, multi-word keywords are stricter.

universal_rules.applies_when: query mentions outstanding payments, unpaid disputes, what's owed, money owed, awaiting payment
universal_rules.rule: IDRE defines 'outstanding payment' as cases in one of 5 specific statuses AND a NOT EXISTS subquery confirming the relevant party has not paid. Statuses: PENDING_PAYMENTS, PENDING_SECOND_PAYMENT, PENDING_ADMINISTRATIVE_CLOSURE, INELIGIBLE, INELIGIBLE_PENDING_ADMIN_FEE. 'Paid' means EXISTS a case_payment_allocation for that partyType joined to payment with direction='INCOMING' AND status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED','COMPLETED'). Party-specific (IP vs NIP).
universal_rules.sql_snippet: WHERE c.status IN ('PENDING_PAYMENTS','PENDING_SECOND_PAYMENT','PENDING_ADMINISTRATIVE_CLOSURE','INELIGIBLE','INELIGIBLE_PENDING_ADMIN_FEE') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM case_payment_allocation cpa JOIN payment p ON p.id = cpa.paymentId WHERE cpa.caseId = c.id AND cpa.partyType = 'INITIATING' AND p.direction = 'INCOMING' AND p.status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED','COMPLETED'))

universal_rules.applies_when: query mentions due dates, deadlines, overdue, urgent, past due, upcoming deadlines
universal_rules.rule: IDRE computes a case's primary due date by COALESCE across 4 columns in this priority: due_date, eligibilityDueDate, paymentDueDate, due_date_until_decision. To filter cases with ANY due date passed/upcoming, OR-test all 4 columns. The dueDateType label is derived from which column is non-null.
universal_rules.sql_snippet: WHERE (c.due_date < NOW() OR c.eligibilityDueDate < NOW() OR c.paymentDueDate < NOW() OR c.due_date_until_decision < NOW()) -- and COALESCE for primary date selection: COALESCE(c.due_date, c.eligibilityDueDate, c.paymentDueDate, c.due_date_until_decision) AS primaryDueDate

universal_rules.applies_when: query mentions CMS payments, CMS revenue, CMS amounts
universal_rules.rule: 'CMS payments' in IDRE = payment.type = 'CASE_PAYMENT'. The bot must NEVER use 'CMS_INVOICE_PAYMENT' or 'CMS_ADMIN_FEE_TRANSFER' as a filter — those enum values do exist but they're not what users mean by 'CMS payment'. 'CMS' is the report context, not the payment type.

universal_rules.applies_when: query mentions payouts to a specific entity (Capitol Bridge, Halo, VeraTru, etc.)
universal_rules.rule: Match payee entities through the bankingSnapshot JSON field on payment, NOT through the organization table. Use LOWER(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(p.bankingSnapshot, '$.accountHolderName'))) OR LOWER(p.recipientName). Always filter direction='OUTGOING' AND status='COMPLETED'.
universal_rules.sql_snippet: WHERE p.direction = 'OUTGOING' AND p.status = 'COMPLETED' AND (LOWER(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(p.bankingSnapshot, '$.accountHolderName'))) LIKE '%capitol bridge%' OR LOWER(p.recipientName) LIKE '%capitol bridge%')

universal_rules.applies_when: query mentions case balance, net balance, money allocated/refunded per case
universal_rules.rule: balance = SUM(allocatedAmount from INCOMING APPROVED/COMPLETED payments) - SUM(refundAmountCents/100 from COMPLETED case_refunds). Use case_refunds table for refunds, NOT case_payment_allocation. Do NOT subtract OUTGOING from INCOMING — refunds live in case_refunds.
universal_rules.sql_snippet: SELECT c.id, (COALESCE(alloc.totalAllocated, 0) - COALESCE(ref.totalRefunded, 0)) AS balance FROM `case` c LEFT JOIN (SELECT cpa.caseId, SUM(cpa.allocatedAmount) AS totalAllocated FROM case_payment_allocation cpa JOIN payment p ON p.id = cpa.paymentId WHERE p.direction='INCOMING' AND p.status IN ('APPROVED','COMPLETED') GROUP BY cpa.caseId) alloc ON alloc.caseId = c.id LEFT JOIN (SELECT cr.caseId, SUM(cr.refundAmountCents)/100.0 AS totalRefunded FROM case_refunds cr WHERE cr.status='COMPLETED' GROUP BY cr.caseId) ref ON ref.caseId = c.id

universal_rules.applies_when: query mentions recent, today, this week, latest, last 7 days, recent activity
universal_rules.rule: Apply a 7-day recency filter for 'recent' / 'this week' / 'latest'. Use :now placeholder so executor can substitute the temporal anchor. Without this, queries return all-time data (e.g., 889K case_actions instead of last-week's 8K).

universal_rules.applies_when: query asks about arbitrators, team performance, assigned-to, staff caseload
universal_rules.rule: When counting cases per user (arbitrator/team queries): use case.assignedToId for the JOIN (NOT closed_by_user_id which is almost always empty). Filter user.role IN ('arbitrator', 'arbitrator-contractor', 'admin-support', 'eligibility-specialist'). Use LEFT JOIN to include 0-case users when listing workload. Active arbitrators come from the user table by role, not from counting case assignments.
universal_rules.sql_snippet: SELECT u.id, u.name, COUNT(c.id) AS case_count FROM `user` u LEFT JOIN `case` c ON c.assignedToId = u.id WHERE u.role IN ('arbitrator','arbitrator-contractor','admin-support','eligibility-specialist') GROUP BY u.id, u.name

universal_rules.applies_when: query mentions daily funds, daily net, net cash position, incoming vs outgoing
universal_rules.rule: Net cash = SUM(amount WHERE direction='INCOMING') - SUM(amount WHERE direction='OUTGOING'), filtered to status IN ('APPROVED','COMPLETED'), grouped by direction for breakdown. NOT 'count of new cases today' — that's a different metric.
universal_rules.sql_snippet: SELECT p.direction, SUM(p.amount) AS total FROM payment p WHERE p.status IN ('APPROVED','COMPLETED') AND DATE(p.createdAt) = DATE(:now) GROUP BY p.direction

universal_rules.rule: Always filter dispute_line_items WHERE status='ACTIVE'. 'REMOVED' rows are soft-deleted and must not appear.

universal_rules.applies_when: query asks about active vs closed cases
universal_rules.rule: Terminal statuses (case is closed): CLOSED_DEFAULT, CLOSED_INITIATING_PARTY, CLOSED_NON_INITIATING_PARTY, CLOSED_ADMINISTRATIVE, CLOSED_SPLIT_DECISION, NOTICE_OF_DISMISSAL_NON_PAYMENT, CLOSED_DEFAULT_IP, CLOSED_DEFAULT_NIP, INELIGIBLE, FINAL_DETERMINATION_RENDERED. Source: config/business_glossary.json terminal-status entry (single source of truth post-Node 3).

universal_rules.applies_when: query mentions an organization name or filters by organization
universal_rules.rule: JOIN organization through the appropriate FK based on context: nonInitiatingPartyOrganizationId for NIP, initiatingPartyOrganizationId for IP, ownedByOrganizationId for owner. When unclear which party, OR across all three FKs.
universal_rules.sql_snippet: JOIN organization nip_org ON nip_org.id = c.nonInitiatingPartyOrganizationId WHERE nip_org.name LIKE '%<name>%'

universal_rules.applies_when: any query listing or counting cases
universal_rules.rule: Always SELECT `case`.shortId AS dispute_number for user-facing displays. Never expose `case`.id (internal UUID, useless to users). When the user searches by 'DISP-XXXXXXX', strip the DISP- prefix: WHERE `case`.shortId = 'XXXXXXX'.
